title: Regulatory Authority Notification Delivery Confirmation (Art. 73)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.25: Regulatory Authority Notification Delivery Confirmation (Art. 73).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.25' running on index=mail sourcetype=o365:messaging OR sourcetype=sendgrid:event — message_id, event, recipient_domain, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) or generic mail gateway logs
spl:
index=mail (sourcetype="o365:messaging" OR sourcetype="sendgrid:event") earliest=-30d recipient_domain="*authority*"
| stats earliest(eval(if(event="queued",_time,null()))) as t_q,
        earliest(eval(if(event="delivered",_time,null()))) as t_d,
        earliest(eval(if(match(event,"bounce|dropped"),_time,null()))) as t_fail
    by message_id
| eval deliver_h=round((t_d-t_q)/3600,3)
| where isnull(t_d) OR isnotnull(t_fail)
| table message_id, t_q, t_d, t_fail, deliver_h
description: Confirms secure notification channel message_id acknowledged by authority mailbox/API within policy window.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes mail and sourcetypes o365:messaging, o365:messaging`, sendgrid:event. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Tag regulatory messages with dedicated header; (2) alert on bounce; (3) retain DKIM pass evidence; (4) legal owns recipient allowlist. Indexes required: `index=mail`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=o365:messaging`, `sourcetype=o365:messaging``, `sourcetype=sendgrid:event`, `sourcetype=sendgrid:event``.
visualization: Table (failed deliveries), Time chart (deliver_h), Single value (undelivered count)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for confirms secure notification channel message_id acknowledged by authority mailbox/api within policy window. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) or generic mail gateway logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=mail` `sourcetype=o365:messaging` OR `sourcetype=sendgrid:event` — message_id, event, recipient_domain.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag regulatory messages with dedicated header; (2) alert on bounce; (3) retain DKIM pass evidence; (4) legal owns recipient allowlist.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mail (sourcetype="o365:messaging" OR sourcetype="sendgrid:event") earliest=-30d recipient_domain="*authority*"
| stats earliest(eval(if(event="queued",_time,null()))) as t_q,
        earliest(eval(if(event="delivered",_time,null()))) as t_d,
        earliest(eval(if(match(event,"bounce|dropped"),_time,null()))) as t_fail
    by message_id
| eval deliver_h=round((t_d-t_q)/3600,3)
| where isnull(t_d) OR isnotnull(t_fail)
| table message_id, t_q, t_d, t_fail, deliver_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**Regulatory Authority Notification Delivery Confirmation (Art. 73) (EU AI Act)** — Confirms secure notification channel message_id acknowledged by authority mailbox/API within policy window.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mail` `sourcetype=o365:messaging` OR `sourcetype=sendgrid:event` — message_id, event, recipient_domain. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) or generic mail gateway logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mail; **sourcetype**: o365:messaging, sendgrid:event. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=mail, sourcetype="o365:messaging", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **message_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **deliver_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(t_d) OR isnotnull(t_fail)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: deliver_h, message_id, t_d, t_fail, t_q.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed deliveries), Time chart (deliver_h), Single value (undelivered count)
